name: mgnl
description: Magnolia CLI is a command line interface (CLI) tool to set up and facilitate light development with Magnolia

name: --help
-h
options.description: Show help for mgnl

subcommands.name: add-availability
subcommands.description: This command makes a component available to a page

args.name: <[module-id:]path-to-component>
args.description: The component you want to make available

args.name: <path-to-page[@area]>
args.description: The page you want to make the component available to

name: -p
--path
options.description: The path to the light module that contains the page template

options.name: -g
options.description: Add this parameter to autogenerate the component instead of providing plain availability

subcommands.name: create-app
subcommands.description: This command creates a content type and an app

args.name: name
args.description: The name of the new app. The name cannot contain spaces

name: -p
--path
options.description: The path to the light module where you want to create the app

name: -P
--prototype
options.description: The name of the prototype variant to create the app

args.name: blockName
args.description: The name of the new block definition. The block name cannot contain spaces

name: -p
--path
options.description: The path to the light module to add the new block to

name: -P
--prototype
options.description: The name of the prototype variant to create the block

subcommands.name: create-component
subcommands.description: This command creates a new component template

args.name: name
args.description: The name of the new component template. The name cannot contain spaces

name: -p
--path
options.description: The path to the light module you want to add the component template to

name: -a
--available
options.description: The page you want to make the component available to

name: -g
--autogenerate
options.description: Add this parameter to autogenerate the component instead of providing plain availability

name: -P
--prototype
options.description: The name of the prototype variant to create the component

subcommands.name: create-content-type
subcommands.description: This command creates a content type

args.name: name
args.description: The name of the new content type. The name cannot contain spaces

options.name: -a
options.description: Creates also a new app that references the content type. With the -a option, the command is functionally equivalent to the create-app command

name: -p
--path
options.description: The path to the light module where you want to create the content type

name: -P
--prototype
options.description: The name of the prototype variant to create the content type

subcommands.name: create-light-module
subcommands.description: This command creates a new light module in the form of a set of empty light module folders

args.name: moduleName
args.description: The name of the new light module. Avoid spaces and special characters since this name is used as folder name

name: -p
--path
options.description: The path of the parent directory for the new light module

name: -P
--prototype
options.description: The name of the prototype variant to create the light module

subcommands.name: create-page
subcommands.description: This command creates a new page template

args.name: templateName
args.description: The name of the new page template. The template name cannot contain spaces

name: -p
--path
options.description: The path to the light module to add the new template to

name: -P
--prototype
options.description: The name of the prototype variant to create the page

subcommands.name: create-virtual-uri
subcommands.description: The command creates a virtual URI mapping configuration file (a YAML definition file) in the light module

args.name: virtualUriName
args.description: The name of the new URI configuration

name: -p
--path
options.description: The path to the light module

name: -f
--formUri
options.description: The pattern to be matched in the requested URI. Enclose the <uri> value in quotes

name: -t
--toUri
options.description: A concrete URI that the request is mapped to. Enclose the <prefix:uri> value in quotes

name: -P
--prototype
options.description: The name of the prototype variant to create the mapping

subcommands.name: customize-local-config
subcommands.description: Run this command to create a local configuration

name: -p
--path
options.description: The path into which the mgnl-cli-prototypes folder and mgnl-cli.json file are installed

subcommands.name: install
subcommands.description: Downloads and installs one or more light modules from npm to the light module directory

args.name: light-module-name
args.description: At least one name of a light module to be downloaded from npm. If you are installing more than one module from the repository, separate the module names with a space

name: -p
--path
options.description: The path to the light-modules folder

subcommands.name: jumpstart
subcommands.description: This command downloads, unpacks and pre-configures a Magnolia Tomcat server together with a specific webapp

name: -p
--path
options.description: The path to the light modules root folder which is observed for changes

name: -m
--magnolia-version
options.description: Use to choose a specific version of the Magnolia webapp. If not provided, the latest stable version of the specified webapp is downloaded

name: -i
--install-sample-module
options.description: If provided, a sample light module under the light modules root folder with the given name is created

name: -s
--snapshot
options.description: Downloads the latest snapshot development version of the specified webapp

name: -w
--webapp
options.description: Use to directly specify which Magnolia webapp you want to install

subcommands.name: tab-completion
subcommands.description: Run the command to install or uninstall autocompletion for Magnolia CLI commands

subcommands.name: search
subcommands.description: Searches for Magnolia-related packages available from npm

args.name: query
args.description: The search query sent to the npm's API. The query can contain multiple terms separated with commas and no spaces

subcommands.name: start
subcommands.description: This command starts up Magnolia and displays the main log file

name: -p
--path
options.description: The path to the apache-tomcat folder

options.name: -d
options.description: Does NOT ignore the open files limit check. The files limit check is ignored by default
